notes: Current public development-line manifest for the 4.1.3.6 compatibility work batch after the 4.1.3.5 release-line hardening slice, continuing preparation for the 4.1.4 AgentTask target.

runtime_event_ownership.plugin_contract: plugins return observations/errors/decisions; core maps them to official RuntimeEvent records

runtime_naming.attempt_index: model-request retry attempt metadata; not an agent turn counter

runtime_capability_contract.skill_capability_needs: SkillsExecutor records selected Skill capability needs in SkillExecutionPlan.capability_needs from SKILL.md guidance, resource indexes, public compatibility, public metadata, and optional model-assisted inference.
runtime_capability_contract.search_provider: Framework Search uses the ddgs package by default, keeps backend configurable through host policy, supports host-approved ddgs refresh before search runs, treats no-result search as a successful empty result, and falls back through configured/default ddgs backends when a selected backend returns no usable parsed result.
runtime_capability_contract.workspace_file_action_owner: Workspace owns file action roots and path boundaries; SkillsExecutor prefers workspace.enable_file_actions(agent, ...) before falling back to agent.enable_workspace_file_actions(...).
runtime_capability_contract.policy_approval_surface: Skills capability approval uses the framework-wide PolicyApproval handler surface: input_timeout_fail by default, fail_closed for pending diagnostics/TriggerFlow gates, auto_approve for tests, input, or registered service-specific handlers.

action_runtime.partial_success_contract: Action results with status=partial_success keep success=True/ok=True and carry diagnostics for degraded providers or fallback recovery. ActionFlow treats partial_success as continuable evidence, not action.failed.
action_runtime.search_partial_success: Built-in Search returns partial_success when earlier ddgs backends fail or return empty parsed results but a later fallback backend returns usable results.
action_runtime.bash_sandbox_model_description: Model-visible bash sandbox action descriptions include the allowed command prefixes, allowed working-directory roots, and timeout when the action is registered or enabled through agent.enable_shell(...).

configuration.default_output_format: Omitted .output(..., format=...) reads prompt.default_output_format from the current settings chain; the global default is json, while explicit format="auto" and per-agent/request prompt.default_output_format="auto" remain available for schema-driven selection.
configuration.tuple_ensure_value_contract: Tuple ensure flags and ensure_keys require paths to resolve to meaningful values: missing keys, None, blank strings, empty wildcard matches, and wildcard matches containing blank required values fail and share the normal retry budget; False and 0 remain valid typed values.

triggerflow.runtime_integrity_contract: TriggerFlow preserves execution-local runtime scope across framework-owned fan-out and chunk-internal emits; batch creates a real fan-out signal and isolates concurrent invocations; chunk-emitted signals carry parent signal identity and aggregation scope for DAG-friendly joins; flow.when([...], mode="and") is the event-list shorthand for multi-dependency joins; empty for_each emits an empty collected result; no-hit match restores its layer before continuing; pause_for(..., resume_to="self") records resume_count and defaults to max_resumes=1; execution.save() includes a versioned checkpoint envelope with durable TriggerFlow system progress, flow definition fingerprint validation, interrupt/resume ledger, resource requirements, lease metadata, and managed execution environment requirements for restart-safe rehydration without serializing live resources; Agently.create_trigger_flow(...) mirrors TriggerFlow(...) for factory-style flow definition creation; flow/execution declare_resource_requirement(...), execution.inspect_rehydration(...), execution.async_rehydrate(...), execution.set_checkpoint_store(...), execution.set_runtime_event_store(...), execution.async_save_checkpoint(...), and idempotent continue_with(..., resume_request_id=...) form the recovery-facing contract; passing runtime_resources={"workspace": workspace} or runtime_resources={"durable_provider": provider} binds compatible checkpoint and RuntimeEvent provider ports automatically; Workspace-backed checkpoints are read through the provider and rehydrated by TriggerFlow for pause/continue, policy-approval waits, and when(..., mode="and") join progress; configured RuntimeEvent stores receive the same canonical RuntimeEvent identities that EventCenter dispatches; execution.async_save_checkpoint(..., require_distributed_provider=True) fails closed unless providers report CAS, lease, range-read, retention, and event-sequence capabilities; async_close runs once, clears transient aggregation scratch state, and removes closed executions from the live registry; execution concurrency is a global handler dispatch limit with permit yielding around awaited internal dispatch.

workspace.durable_provider_contract: Workspace remains the public durable information boundary and now exposes typed provider seams for stable reference envelopes, bounded reads, stream reads, RuntimeEvent storage, file-policy metadata, evidence links, retention anchors, checkpoint lookup, backend-provider registration, and backend capability reporting. Default Agents expose agent.workspace as a lazy Workspace foundation capability; the local filesystem plus SQLite backend materializes only when Workspace storage, checkpoint, evidence, read, or file-area behavior is actually used. Application-owned shared Workspaces are created through Agently.create_workspace(...) and explicitly bound to Agents, TriggerFlow executions, or service workers when they must share task information; separate Workspaces exchange information only through application-level search/read/write/link logic. Third-party Workspace backends can be passed directly to agent.use_workspace(backend) or registered through Agently.workspace.register_backend_provider(name, factory) and selected with agent.use_workspace(root, provider=name, provider_options={...}); unregistered providers fail fast. TriggerFlow can bind compatible Workspace or durable-provider runtime resources to checkpoint and RuntimeEvent provider ports automatically; provider checkpoints can be read back and passed to TriggerFlow rehydration for pause/continue, policy-approval waits, and when(..., mode="and") join progress. A protocol-level remote audit provider proof covers custom WorkspaceBackend replacement across Agent binding, provider registration, TriggerFlow checkpoint/RuntimeEvent ports, distributed capability checks, and AgentExecution evidence links. AgentExecution.async_record_workspace(...) writes requested checkpoints through the Workspace checkpoint-store port and records evidence links between AgentExecution records and checkpoints. The AgentTask task strategy writes its planning, observation, verification, checkpoint, and evidence-link records through Workspace provider ports while keeping strategy decisions in AgentExecution/AgentTask. Workspace does not own TriggerFlow execution policy, AgentExecution strategy selection, approval decisions, DAG readiness, DevTools projection truth, live resource serialization, or cross-space messaging/replication.
deferred: production external-provider distributed recovery operational guarantees
resumable AgentExecution strategies beyond the current AgentTask task-strategy evidence slice

agent_turn_request_scope.contract: Non-always Agent quick prompt calls create an isolated AgentExecution draft; AgentExecution owns execution-local ModelRequest prompt state and returns AgentExecutionResult. Prompt config .execution is the recommended one-run prompt section. AgentTurn/create_turn/set_turn_prompt remain compatibility aliases with deprecation warnings; Agent-level persistent state remains on define(...), always=True, set_agent_prompt(...), and stable setup APIs.
agent_turn_request_scope.compatibility_policy: Expression-local chaining is the recommended execution-scoped shape. Multi-statement setup should capture execution = agent.create_execution() or use agent.input(...). set_turn_prompt(...) and set_request_prompt(...) remain compatibility aliases for set_execution_prompt(...); prompt config .turn and .request remain compatibility aliases for .execution; explicit low-level agent.create_request()/agent.request builders remain available for direct ModelRequest use.

response_stream_type_surface.contract: Common generator and handler type annotations are exposed through agently and agently.types.data. Result-named aliases are the recommended root-level public names: delta yields str, instant/streaming_parse yields StreamingData with is_complete, specific/all yield event-tagged result messages, AgentExecution streams yield AgentExecutionStreamData, and direct Skills stream handlers receive SkillRuntimeStreamItem dictionaries. Response-named aliases are compatibility-only aliases under agently.types.data and are not re-exported from the agently root.
response_stream_type_surface.compatibility_policy: StreamingData.is_complete remains the canonical instant/streaming_parse completion field. Response-named type aliases remain compatibility-only aliases in agently.types.data and are deprecated in favor of Result-named aliases; they will be removed in Agently 4.2.

model_response_result_facade.contract: get_result() is the recommended reusable facade for text, data, metadata, and streaming readers. Agent quick prompt chains return AgentExecutionResult; direct ModelRequest calls return ModelResponseResult. get_response() remains a compatibility alias. ModelResponseResult.result points to itself so older response.result reader chains continue to work.
model_response_result_facade.compatibility_policy: Recommended documentation, examples, and Skills guidance use result = agent.input(...).output(...).get_result(); data = result.get_data(). Code that already calls get_response() and then reader methods or response.result reader chains remains supported. Direct ModelResponse construction is deprecated and will be removed in Agently 4.2.

agent_execution_task_loop.contract: The 4.1.3.6 task-loop contract is a narrow single-task, single-Agent-owner slice carried by AgentExecution. agent.create_task(...) and agent.create_task_loop(...) return task-strategy AgentExecution drafts, not a separate recommended AgentTask execution owner. Consumers read final data, text, streams, metadata, and task refs through AgentExecutionResult or the execution stream/meta facade. Completion acceptance requires model-owned verification plus conservative host guards; completed means accepted=true and artifact_status=accepted, while max_iterations can return accepted=false and artifact_status=partial. The task strategy writes Workspace checkpoints through the checkpoint-store port and records task evidence relationships with Workspace evidence links. AgentExecutionResult resume is reserved and reports supported=false until a resumable strategy lands.

current_slice: explicitly enabled Actions, Skills, or Dynamic Task candidates as step capabilities
model-owned planning, verification, and replan decisions
host guards for missing criteria, risky action evidence, approval-required actions, and missing final deliverables
structured task refs, diagnostics, progress/snapshot stream items, result facade consumption, and provider-backed Workspace evidence links
deferred: durable pause/resume beyond the reserved result surface

agent_execution_task_loop.compatibility_policy: New documentation and examples should keep AgentExecutionResult as the common consumption surface. Task-style compatibility readers may remain callable, but they should not be promoted as a second recommended public lifecycle. Future 4.1.3.7 AgentTaskLoop hardening must strengthen the task strategy behind AgentExecution without changing this public ownership boundary.

image.attachment_contract: Builds text plus image_url rich-content blocks and keeps attachment as the low-level passthrough.
